input.background: An important historical case-control completed by Doll and Hill found that 647 of the 649 lung cancer cases were smokers. In contrast, 622 of 649 controls smoked.
input.question: Calculate the odds ratio associated with smoking.

output.answer:
$$
\widehat{O R}-\frac{647 \cdot 27}{622 \cdot 2}=14.04
$$

This suggests that the smokers had 14 times the risk of nonsmokers.

lung_cancer_cases.description: Total number of lung cancer cases in the study.

smokers_with_lung_cancer.description: Number of smokers among the lung cancer cases.

controls.description: Total number of control cases in the study.

smokers_in_controls.description: Number of smokers among the control cases.
